audit_status.review_note: Genealogical chapter reviewed for adam/enosh language, divine image echo, naming, walking with God, mortality formula, and Noah wordplay. Final source-critical review may refine details.

verses.restored: This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day the Divine created the human, in the likeness of the Divine he made him.

familiar.text: This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;

notes.title: Book of generations
notes.body: Sefer toledot introduces a written or formal lineage record. Adam can mean the human, humanity, or the named ancestor depending on context.

verses.restored: Male and female he created them, and he blessed them and called their name Human in the day they were created.

familiar.text: Male and female created he them; and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.

notes.title: Their name Human
notes.body: The text says their name Adam. Human preserves that adam here includes male and female together.

verses.restored: Adam lived one hundred thirty years and fathered a son in his likeness, according to his image, and called his name Seth.

familiar.text: And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his image; and called his name Seth:

notes.title: Image passed on
notes.body: Image and likeness language echoes Genesis 1, now applied to human generation.

verses.restored: The days of Adam after he fathered Seth were eight hundred years, and he fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days that Adam lived were nine hundred thirty years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years: and he died.

notes.title: And he died
notes.body: The repeated mortality formula grounds the genealogy in the consequence of Genesis 3.

verses.restored: Seth lived one hundred five years and fathered Enosh.

familiar.text: And Seth lived an hundred and five years, and begat Enos:

verses.restored: After Seth fathered Enosh, Seth lived eight hundred seven years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Seth lived after he begat Enos eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days of Seth were nine hundred twelve years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years: and he died.

verses.restored: Enosh lived ninety years and fathered Kenan.

familiar.text: And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan:

verses.restored: After Enosh fathered Kenan, he lived eight hundred fifteen years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Enos lived after he begat Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days of Enosh were nine hundred five years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years: and he died.

verses.restored: Kenan lived seventy years and fathered Mahalalel.

familiar.text: And Cainan lived seventy years, and begat Mahalaleel:

verses.restored: After Kenan fathered Mahalalel, he lived eight hundred forty years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Cainan lived after he begat Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days of Kenan were nine hundred ten years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years: and he died.

verses.restored: Mahalalel lived sixty-five years and fathered Jared.

familiar.text: And Mahalaleel lived sixty and five years, and begat Jared:

verses.restored: After Mahalalel fathered Jared, he lived eight hundred thirty years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Mahalaleel lived after he begat Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred ninety-five years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred ninety and five years: and he died.

verses.restored: Jared lived one hundred sixty-two years and fathered Enoch.

familiar.text: And Jared lived an hundred sixty and two years, and he begat Enoch:

verses.restored: After Jared fathered Enoch, he lived eight hundred years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Jared lived after he begat Enoch eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty-two years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty and two years: and he died.

verses.restored: Enoch lived sixty-five years and fathered Methuselah.

familiar.text: And Enoch lived sixty and five years, and begat Methuselah:

verses.restored: Enoch walked with the Divine after he fathered Methuselah three hundred years, and he fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methuselah three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:

notes.title: Walked with the Divine
notes.body: Halakh with Elohim marks Enoch as living in ongoing divine fellowship. The usual death formula is interrupted in his story.

verses.restored: All the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty-five years.

familiar.text: And all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty and five years:

verses.restored: Enoch walked with the Divine, and he was not, for the Divine took him.

familiar.text: And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.

notes.title: He was not
notes.body: Unlike the repeated and he died formula, Enoch is taken by the Divine. The text is brief and mysterious.

verses.restored: Methuselah lived one hundred eighty-seven years and fathered Lamech.

familiar.text: And Methuselah lived an hundred eighty and seven years, and begat Lamech:

verses.restored: After Methuselah fathered Lamech, he lived seven hundred eighty-two years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Methuselah lived after he begat Lamech seven hundred eighty and two years, and begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty-nine years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty and nine years: and he died.

verses.restored: Lamech lived one hundred eighty-two years and fathered a son.

familiar.text: And Lamech lived an hundred eighty and two years, and begat a son:

verses.restored: And he called his name Noah, saying, This one will comfort us from our work and from the painful toil of our hands, from the ground that the Eternal has cursed.

familiar.text: And he called his name Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands, because of the ground which the LORD hath cursed.

notes.title: Noah wordplay
notes.body: Noah is connected here with comfort/rest, echoing the human longing for relief from cursed-ground toil.

verses.restored: After Lamech fathered Noah, he lived five hundred ninety-five years and fathered sons and daughters.

familiar.text: And Lamech lived after he begat Noah five hundred ninety and five years, and begat sons and daughters:

verses.restored: All the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy-seven years, and he died.

familiar.text: And all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy and seven years: and he died.

notes.title: Seven hundred seventy-seven
notes.body: The number stands out after Genesis 4's Lamech and his seventy-sevenfold vengeance, though this is a different Lamech in Seth's line.

verses.restored: Noah was five hundred years old, and Noah fathered Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

familiar.text: And Noah was five hundred years old: and Noah begat Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

notes.title: Transition to flood story
notes.body: The genealogy closes by introducing Noah's three sons, who structure the post-flood world.
